localContext: Mamaroneck's beautiful waterfront properties and historic homes along the Boston Post Road require special attention when it comes to fabric pest protection. faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Mamaroneck home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small irregular holes in wool, silk, or cashmere clothing, webbing in closet corners, adult moths flying near wardrobes, and larvae casings in fabric folds. You may also notice damage to area rugs, upholstered furniture, or stored blankets. Our Mamaroneck technicians provide free inspections to confirm infestations.

faqs.question: What is included in your clothes moth control service?
faqs.answer: Our comprehensive service includes a thorough inspection of closets, storage areas, and upholstered furniture, identification of moth species and breeding sites, targeted treatment using safe insecticides and pheromone traps, removal of larvae and eggs, and prevention recommendations. We also offer follow-up visits to ensure complete elimination.

faqs.question: Why does clothes moth control cost between $550 and $5,000?
faqs.answer: Pricing depends on the severity of the infestation, the size of your home, the number of affected areas, and whether multiple treatments are needed. A minor infestation in one closet may cost around $550, while severe infestations affecting multiple rooms, requiring deep cleaning and repeated treatments, can reach $5,000. We provide detailed estimates after inspection.

faqs.question: How can I prevent clothes moths from returning to my Mamaroneck home?
faqs.answer: Regular vacuuming of closets and storage areas, storing seasonal clothing in airtight containers, dry cleaning garments before long-term storage, using cedar blocks or lavender sachets, and maintaining low humidity levels all help prevent re-infestation. Our technicians provide customized prevention plans based on your home's specific conditions and proximity to the Long Island Sound.
